summary: Your LT test today puts you at 4:24/km @ 178 bpm — a useful anchor point, though with no 30-day baseline to compare against, it's a standalone marker for now; track the next test carefully so you can start reading directional change. Volume has stepped back 13% over the last four weeks (113 km vs 131 km prior), which looks deliberate given the 44 km effort on June 8 — that kind of load warrants a consolidation phase. Yesterday's 11.7 km at 5:59/km and HR 143 reads as genuine recovery work, sitting well below the June 27 session where you were pushing 4:49/km at HR 161, likely threshold-adjacent territory. Strength frequency is light at 3 sessions across the last four weeks; with 33 YTD that's a reasonable overall count, but if you're heading into a build block, tightening that cadence would be worth prioritising. Rest-day step count averaging 11,080 suggests solid background activity without overdoing non-run load.
